big_picture: The Supreme Court has taken over cases from the Allahabad High Court due to its failure to pronounce a verdict six years after reserving judgment. Gold and silver prices in India rose on March 5 due to Middle East tensions, with gold trading at ₹1.62 lakh/10g and silver at ₹2.69 lakh/kg on MCX. The Kerala High Court will decide whether the state can send "Thank You" messages to donors of the Chief Minister's Disaster Relief Fund. Air India plans to operate more flights to Toronto, Frankfurt, and Paris amid high demand.

articles.title: IEX Power Trade Surges 30% YoY to 12,550 MU in February
articles.summary: The Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) recorded a total electricity-traded volume of 12,550 million units (MU) in February 2026, a 30.4% year-over-year (YoY) increase, and its highest daily average volume of 448 MU. articles.title: Rupee hits record low beyond 92/$1 amid geopolitical tensions; RBI steps in to limit slide
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a record low of 92.15 against the dollar, mirroring a surge in equity fear gauges. This depreciation is attributed to West Asian war-induced oil and gas supply disruptions, prompting a flight to safe-haven dollar assets. Central bank interventions helped arrest steeper losses, but the rupee faces continued depreciation pressure.

articles.title: Crude shock: After 65% annual surge, Iran-Israel war raises margin fears for BPCL, IOC and HPCL. Time to sell?

Experts advise investors to book profits and consider a sell-on-rise strategy as crude prices are expected to move higher, negatively affecting OMC stocks.

articles.title: Supreme Court Directs Husband's Employer To Deposit Monthly Maintenance To Wife's Account After Deducting From His Salary
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ordered the husband's employer to deduct ₹25,000 monthly from his salary and transfer it to his estranged wife for maintenance. The court took this step after the husband failed to comply with earlier orders. This ruling sets a precedent for enforcing maintenance payments through employer deductions, ensuring compliance with family court directives.

A bench of Justice JB Pardiwala and Justice KV Viswanathan passed the direction after noting that the husband had failed to comply with earlier orders and had not paid any maintenance despite living separately from his wife since 2022. articles.title: ROC imposes Penalty for Filing PAS-3 with Incomplete Allottee Details
articles.summary: The Registrar of Companies (ROC) penalized a company for filing PAS-3 with incomplete allottee details, including missing PAN and email IDs. This violates Rule 14(6) of the Companies Act and attracts penalties under Section 450. The ruling reinforces compliance requirements for corporate filings in India.
articles.raw_summary: ROC held that failure to disclose PAN and email IDs of allottees in the PAS-3 attachment violates Rule 14(6), attracting penalty under Section 450 of the Companies Act. The post ROC imposes Penalty for Filing PAS-3 with Incomplete Allottee Details appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Why Chhattisgarh’s midday meal workers’ have not given up their fight for better pay
articles.summary: Midday meal workers in Chhattisgarh, who earn less than Rs 70 a day, went on a two-month strike demanding a minimum wage of Rs 350. Their work is vital for children's nutrition, and the strike highlights the need for better pay and working conditions for such workers. This issue could prompt policy action on wages for public sector employees.
articles.raw_summary: Their work is crucial for children’s wellbeing, but they earn less than Rs 70 a day. A two-month strike sought to secure them a daily wage of at least Rs 350.

articles.raw_summary: ‘Despite repeated requests to conduct safety audits of such companies, no concrete action has been taken,’ Vijay Wadettiwar said in the Assembly.

articles.title: Recovery Of RERA Compensation Cannot Be Put On Hold Due To Subsequent Vesting Of Land In State: HP High Court
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court has held that recovery proceedings initiated pursuant to final order of the Real Estate Regulatory Authority cannot be stalled merely because the project land subsequently vested in the State Government under separate revenue proceedings. The Court further clarified that once a RERA order granting monetary relief attained finality, authorities were bound to enforce it through the statutory recovery mechanisms available under revenue law. Justice Jyotsna Rewal Dua

articles.title: Enforcement, yes — but with accountability: Understanding arrest provisions in GST law
articles.summary: The article explains the arrest provisions in India's GST law, emphasizing that while tax evasion cases justify such measures, arrests should be made with accountability. Former CBIC chairman Najib Shah highlights that legal grounds alone do not mandate arrests, ensuring a balanced approach to enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: Given the large-scale detection of tax evasion cases, which have a multiplier effect on the economy and may even fund anti-national activities, arrest provisions are necessary in fiscal laws. However, the Board’s circular makes it clear that the mere existence of legal grounds for arrest does not automatically mandate an arrest, writes former CBIC chairman Najib Shah.

articles.title: Middle East conflict poses near-term challenges to Indian economy: RBI MPC member
articles.summary: Middle East conflict presents short-term challenges for India's economy, including oil price hikes and export disruptions, but is unlikely to derail long-term growth. An RBI panel member highlighted the need for coordinated fiscal and monetary policies to achieve an 8% GDP growth trajectory, supported by manufacturing and services. Inflation remains benign, offering a 'Goldilocks' scenario.

Inflation remains benign, offering a 'Goldilocks' scenario.

articles.title: 7 out of last 8 mainboard IPOs delivered negative listing gains. What is going wrong with India’s IPO market?
articles.summary: India’s IPO market is showing signs of cooling, with seven of the last eight mainboard listings delivering negative debut gains. Weak broader markets, stretched valuations, and softer investor appetite have weighed on listing performance. Analysts say cautious sentiment and declining subscription levels suggest investors are becoming more selective about new public offerings.

articles.title: India VIX eases 17% after massive 52% flare up in just 2 sessions amid Middle East war. What should traders do?
articles.summary: India VIX cooled on Thursday after a sharp 52% surge in just two sessions triggered by escalating Middle East tensions. Despite the pullback, volatility remains elevated near 19, prompting experts to advise traders to stay cautious, hedge portfolios and avoid aggressive positions until greater clarity emerges on the Iran–Israel–US conflict.

articles.title: Markets in turmoil, but fundamentals intact: Ajay Bagga advises patience amid global uncertainty
articles.summary: Indian markets are navigating a turbulent phase. Geopolitical tensions and volatile commodity prices are impacting investor sentiment. Defence sector presents a long-term opportunity. Investors should prepare but not rush.

However, rising crude oil prices and foreign investor selling continue to exert pressure. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are also a significant factor influencing the currency's performance.

articles.title: Disallowance of Insurance Expenses Set Aside Because IRDA Limits Do Not Automatically Bar Tax Deduction
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that insurance companies cannot be denied tax deductions for management expenses just because IRDA regulations require them to be reallocated. The decision clarifies that IRDA limits do not automatically override tax laws, ensuring compliance with both regulatory frameworks. This ruling impacts insurance firms' tax planning and financial reporting.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal ruled that reallocating management expenses to the profit and loss account under IRDA regulations does not violate law and therefore cannot justify disallowance under the Income-tax Act. The post Disallowance of Insurance Expenses Set Aside Because IRDA Limits Do Not Automatically Bar Tax Deduction appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Application Rejected as DRT Lacks Jurisdiction Over Recovery Dispute Between Financial Institutions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the Debt Recovery Tribunal (DRT) lacks jurisdiction over disputes between financial institutions regarding unpaid dues. The court upheld that Section 11 of the SARFAESI Act mandates arbitration for such disputes. This decision impacts how banks and financial institutions resolve recovery disputes, ensuring compliance with the law.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal applied the Supreme Court’s ruling that Section 11 of the SARFAESI Act provides a mandatory arbitration mechanism for disputes between banks or financial institutions involving unpaid dues. The post Application Rejected as DRT Lacks Jurisdiction Over Recovery Dispute Between Financial Institutions appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Indian government has reaffirmed its stance on social media content takedown deadlines, prioritizing citizen protection in the digital space. articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that a decree of divorce cannot be granted merely on the basis of WhatsApp chats without proper proof through evidence. The Court observed that allegations of cruelty under Section 13(1)(i-a) of the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955 must be established through legally admissible evidence, and the opposing party must be given an opportunity to rebut the material relied upon. articles.title: New Section 74A vs Old Sections 73 & 74 – Boon or Burden for Bona Fide GST Taxpayers?
articles.summary: The Indian government has introduced Section 74A of the GST law, extending the time limit for issuing demand notices to 42 months from the previous three-year limit for non-fraud cases. This change increases compliance risks for genuine taxpayers despite procedural simplifications. The move is part of ongoing GST reforms and has significant implications for businesses and tax compliance.

This is longer than the earlier three-year limit for non-fraud cases, increasing compliance exposure for bona fide taxpayers despite procedural simplification. articles.title: Reassessment Valid Where Loose Papers and Purchaser’s Statement Indicate On-Money: ITAT Ahmedabad

The ruling relied on documentary evidence and the principle of human probabilities to validate the addition of unaccounted sale consideration as short-term capital gains. This decision reinforces tax authorities' ability to scrutinize transactions where discrepancies exist.

articles.title: Service sector activity slightly eases in February; exports pick up: PMI
articles.summary: India's services sector saw a slight slowdown in February, with new business growth at a 13-month low and operating costs at a two-and-a-half-year high. Despite this, overall services activity remained robust, supported by strong domestic demand and international sales, leading to increased optimism for the coming year.

articles.title: India's services growth moderated in February as cost pressures mount, PMI shows
articles.summary: India's services sector growth slowed in February, with new business rising at its slowest pace in over a year due to intensifying competition. Despite this, international sales surged, and firms passed on rising costs, primarily from food, energy, and labor, to customers. Overall private sector growth accelerated, driven by a brighter outlook and increased hiring.

articles.title: India-UK trade deal faces challenges amid West Asia conflict

The government is closely monitoring the situation.

India is taking emergency trade support measures to ensure continuity of essential imports.

articles.title: NHAI awards 111 km-long Odisha Ring Road project to three concessionaires
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India has awarded a major project to build the Bhubaneswar Bypass. This 111 km, six-lane ring road will connect Rameshwar to Tangi. articles.title: Qatar declares force majeure, gas supplies to Indian industry cut amid Iranian strikes
articles.summary: India faces significant energy disruption as Qatar halts liquefied natural gas deliveries. An Iranian drone strike has forced Qatar to declare force majeure. This halt impacts up to 40 percent of India's gas supplies. Industries are seeing cuts, while CNG and piped gas networks are prioritized. Companies are seeking alternative supplies in a firming spot market.

Faster dispute resolution is also key.

articles.title: India's current account deficit widens in third quarter
articles.summary: India's current account deficit widened to $13.2 billion in Q3, primarily due to a larger merchandise trade deficit. However, this was an improvement from the revised Q2 deficit. Net services receipts increased, while primary income account outgo dipped, and personal remittances rose.

However, this was an improvement from the revised Q2 deficit. Net services receipts increased, while primary income account outgo dipped, and personal remittances rose.

articles.title: Forex reserves rise on gold, dollar valuation gains despite balance of payments pressure: RBI
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a nominal rise of USD 19.4 billion in April-December 2025, primarily due to valuation gains from higher gold prices and a weaker dollar. However, excluding these valuation effects, reserves declined by USD 30.8 billion on a balance of payments basis, indicating underlying external flow pressures.
articles.raw_summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a nominal rise of USD 19.4 billion in April-December 2025, primarily due to valuation gains from higher gold prices and a weaker dollar. However, excluding these valuation effects, reserves declined by USD 30.8 billion on a balance of payments basis, indicating underlying external flow pressures.

articles.title: India’s current account deficit widens to $13.2 billion in Q3 FY26
articles.summary: India's current account deficit increased to $13.2 billion in the October to December quarter of 2025-26. This is a rise from the previous year's deficit. The Reserve Bank of India reported this widening. A larger merchandise trade gap primarily caused this increase. Services exports and remittances continued to show strength.

The Reserve Bank of India reported this widening. A larger merchandise trade gap primarily caused this increase. Services exports and remittances continued to show strength.

articles.title: Government reviews trade risks from West Asia tensions, vows steps to keep cargo flowing
articles.summary: India's government met with trade and logistics experts to address the impact of escalating West Asian tensions on exports and imports. Discussions focused on potential disruptions to cargo flows, shipping routes, and costs. The government assured all measures to ensure continuity and protect exporter interests, especially for MSMEs.

articles.title: Industrial output growth cools to 4.8% in January
articles.summary: India's industrial output growth slowed to 4.8 percent in January. Manufacturing and electricity generation showed steady expansion. Basic metals, motor vehicles, and construction goods were key drivers. Consumer durables saw growth, but non-durables contracted. This indicates mixed demand conditions across sectors.

Essential imports and strategic sectors hang in the balance, with broader economic fallout likely if tensions rise.

articles.title: India manufacturing growth hits four-month high in February, PMI shows
articles.summary: India’s factory activity grew at its fastest pace in four months in February, driven by strong domestic demand, though export growth slowed to a 17-month low, a survey showed. The HSBC India Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index rose to 56.9 in February from 55.4 in January, remaining above the 50 mark that signals expansion.

articles.title: Israel-Iran war's 'Strait' fight can damage one of India's well-built firewalls
articles.summary: Israel-Iran conflict India impact: India confronts fresh economic risks as conflict in West Asia intensifies. The killing of Iran's Supreme Leader and subsequent retaliation have heightened fears of oil supply disruptions. This could lead to higher crude prices, impacting domestic inflation and the Indian Rupee. Policymakers are closely monitoring the situation, as the volatile geopolitical landscape introduces significant uncertainty for the nation's economy.

articles.title: Oil, trade risks for India amid Iran-Israel war

The Strait of Hormuz, vital for energy imports, remains a key chokepoint.

articles.title: Can India’s lawmakers step up global outreach through new friendship groups
articles.summary: For the first time, a concerted effort is underway to build institutional links between Indian parliamentarians and their counterparts abroad. This week, Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla announced the formation of Parliamentary Friendship Groups with 64 countries across continents, with more to follow. The move is a welcome step, giving elected representatives a direct platform to share Indian perspectives with lawmakers overseas, writes Political Analyst K V Prasad.

articles.title: Maharashtra transporters to launch statewide ‘chakka jam’ from March 5 over e-challan dispute
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Transport Action Committee (MTAC) plans a statewide road blockade (chakka jam) starting March 5 to protest the government's lack of action on e-challan disputes. articles.summary: The central government has allocated ₹360 crore to Kerala to support the state's deregulation efforts. This initiative aims to simplify procedures for businesses, especially MSMEs, by reducing regulatory constraints on resources like land, labor, and infrastructure. The move is expected to boost ease of doing business in the state.

articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh plans to set up 10 medical colleges under a public-private partnership (PPP) model within two years, as announced by the state's Irrigation Minister. articles.summary: In response to rising tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, where Iran has firmly declared its authority, Russia is set to increase its oil shipments to both China and India. The US Navy is on alert, ready to protect tanker routes through this vital corridor.

articles.title: Trump says US Navy could escort energy tankers through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Former US President Donald Trump has suggested that the US Navy could escort energy tankers through the Strait of Hormuz to ensure the free flow of global energy supplies. This statement comes amid tensions in the region and could affect India's energy security, as the country relies heavily on oil imports passing through the strait.
articles.raw_summary: In a post on Truth Social on Tuesday, Trump said the United States would ensure the continued movement of global energy supplies through the Gulf.

articles.title: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals declares force majeure on gasoline export cargoes for March, April
articles.summary: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals, a state-run refiner, has declared force majeure on gasoline export cargoes for March and April, citing uncontrollable circumstances. The refinery exports 40% of its refined fuel, and this disruption could affect fuel supply chains and trade. The situation may prompt policy discussions on energy security or trade regulations.
articles.raw_summary: The state-run refiner, which exports 40% of its refined fuel, cited circumstances beyond its control for the contractual inability to fulfill orders.

articles.title: India says oil stocks enough to absorb short-term shocks

articles.title: Industrial users stare at propane, natural gas shortage after Qatar halted production
articles.summary: Qatar has halted its propane and natural gas production, leading to a shortage in India. This affects industrial customers, but household supplies remain unaffected. The situation is being closely monitored, and global energy prices and shipping costs are rising.
articles.raw_summary: This impacts industrial customers, though household piped natural gas supplies remain unaffected. Companies are monitoring the situation closely. Global energy prices and shipping costs are also rising.

articles.title: Oil prices to fall after Iran operation ends, says Trump amid surge due to Middle East conflict
articles.summary: The conflict in the Middle East is disrupting oil shipments, causing global shipping rates to rise. This may lead to higher oil prices, which could impact India's energy sector. The situation is being closely watched, with concerns about the potential consequences for global oil supplies.
articles.raw_summary: The conflict is disrupting vital oil and gas shipments in the Middle East. Tankers are avoiding key waterways, increasing global shipping rates. Concerns are high as Iran issues warnings regarding passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Explained: How US-Israel war with Iran could hit India’s oil, trade and energy security
articles.summary: As tensions escalate following the US-Israel strikes against Iran, India faces potential disruptions in oil imports and trade. With nearly 90% of its crude sourced abroad, the impact on energy prices and key exports such as basmati rice could be profound.

articles.title: Oxford museum set to return 500-year-old bronze statue taken from Tamil Nadu temple
articles.summary: A 16th-century bronze statue of Saint Tirumankai Alvar, taken from a Tamil Nadu temple, is returning to India from the UK. Acquired by the Ashmolean Museum in 1967, its provenance became questionable after a 2019 discovery. Following an investigation and a formal claim by India, the museum has agreed to its repatriation, highlighting a commitment to ethical collections.

articles.title: West Asia crisis: 1,500 flights cancelled in four days, Air India to add capacity on key long routes
articles.summary: Airlines are intensifying evacuation efforts with special flights as Middle East air travel disruptions continue for the fourth day. Indian carriers planned 58 flights on Wednesday, with IndiGo and Air India increasing capacity on long-haul routes. Airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi are handling limited repatriation flights, while services in Bahrain, Doha, and Kuwait remain suspended.

articles.title: ‘I have seen the missile’: Indian woman from first evacuation flight recounts tense days in Dubai
articles.summary: An Indian national returning from Dubai described scenes of panic, missile alerts, and sleepless nights as regional tensions escalated. Saumya Khandelwal recounted witnessing missile activity and emergency alarms, forcing her to seek shelter in a hotel basement. Despite the fear, she highlighted the cooperation of authorities and locals in Dubai, and the support from the Indian Embassy.

articles.title: Bihar CM Nitish Kumar likely to head to Rajya Sabha: JDU sources
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has announced the schedule for the upcoming biennial elections to the Council of States (Rajya Sabha) to fill 37 seats across 10 states, as the terms of the current members end in April 2026, according to a press note. The seats are set to fall vacant as the terms of members elected from Maharashtra, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh and Telangana conclude next month.

6L kids born with anomalies per year but no national registry to track them
articles.summary: India faces a critical gap in tracking nearly 6 lakh babies born annually with congenital anomalies, as these conditions increasingly contribute to child mortality. To address this, Smile Train India and Birth Defects Research Foundation launched BIND, advocating for a National Birth Anomalies Registry to improve data, identify risk factors, and guide health planning for better prevention and care.

articles.title: Ravi Shankar to head Lok Sabha privileges panel
articles.summary: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has formed the committee of privileges. This important panel will examine complaints of breach of privilege. BJP MP Ravi Shankar Prasad will lead the 15-member committee. The panel includes members from both the ruling NDA and opposition parties. A complaint against Rahul Gandhi is pending Speaker's decision.

articles.title: US, Israel attacks Iran: AIMPLB urges UN to take steps for ceasefire
articles.summary: The All India Muslim Personal Law Board has strongly condemned the US-Israel's actions against Iran, calling for immediate international intervention to secure a ceasefire. articles.title: Oil companies may cushion impact of spike - report
articles.summary: Oil marketing companies are absorbing recent crude oil price spikes, creating a buffer against inflation and GDP impacts. Despite a 16.8% rise in Brent crude, a full pass-through to consumers is unlikely, with OMCs expected to manage the increase through their margins. This strategy helps cushion India's economy from geopolitical shocks affecting its heavy reliance on imported oil.
